subject: Geography
title: Krill: The Polar Food Chain
description: A 60-second lesson on Krill: The Polar Food Chain.

hook.text: Antarctic krill weigh just 2 grams each, but their total biomass exceeds all humans combined.

buildup.text: These tiny crustaceans feed on algae growing under sea ice.

discovery.text: Whales, seals, penguins, and fish all depend on krill—it's the foundation of the Antarctic food web.

twist.text: Krill populations have declined 80% since the 1970s as sea ice retreats.

climax.text: If krill collapse, every species above them in the food chain collapses too.

punchline.text: The entire Antarctic ecosystem rests on the back of a 2-gram shrimp.

quiz.question: Why are krill so important to Antarctica's ecosystem?
options: They are the foundation of the entire food web
They produce most of the region's oxygen
